extraction.one_line_summary: MARCZYŃSKI Tomasz won from a 20-rider breakaway after CONTADOR Alberto's attack on Alto de la Garbella (40km out) split favorites but no one gained significant time.
tactical_patterns: CONTADOR Alberto attacking on Alto de la Garbella second-category climb 40km out signals early-stage aggression after recovering from previous weakness.
Break never exceeded three minutes despite 20 riders suggests peloton controlled tempo precisely to prevent dangerous GC riders gaining time.
VAN GARDEREN Tejay crashed on descent after being distanced on climb, revealing vulnerability when isolated without teammates on technical descents.
CHAVES Esteban regaining contact on descent demonstrates critical descending skill compensating for uphill weakness in multi-climb stages.

rider_signals.MARCZYŃSKI Tomasz: positive: won stage from breakaway showing opportunistic racing when GC teams neutralize each other
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: positive: attacked decisively on Alto de la Garbella after showing weakness in Andorra, rapid recovery suggests peaking pattern
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: matched CONTADOR's attack on Alto de la Garbella maintaining GC control without expending excessive energy
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: dropped on climb then crashed on descent losing 17 seconds, second overall but showing isolation without team support
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: neutral: initially distanced on Alto de la Garbella but rejoined on descent, descending strength compensates climbing deficit
rider_signals.DE LA CRUZ David: negative: distanced when CONTADOR attacked suggesting inability to follow explosive accelerations on second-category climbs
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: distanced on Alto de la Garbella alongside VAN GARDEREN indicating vulnerability to sustained tempo increases
rider_signals.PANTANO Jarlinson: positive: supported CONTADOR's attack on final climb showing Tinkoff team depth for aggressive racing
